# UC-22.52.23 · SOCI Rule 10 annual report content audit — evidence section-by-section completeness against CIRMP Rules 4-9

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Audit &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Beyond just lodging the yearly report, we make sure each of the rule chapters (Rules 4 through 9) is properly filled in: enough words, evidence links, and signed off by the board. If a section is empty or too thin, we ring the bell so the writer can fix it before the deadline.*

---

## Description

Audits the structural completeness of the Rule 10 annual report by CIRMP rule: every CIRMP rule 4–9 has a section, the section meets a minimum word-count, includes evidence links, and is signed off by the board.

## Value

Rule 10 lodgement (UC-22.52.22) only proves the report exists; this UC proves it has substantive content. Auditors increasingly request to see the report's evidence-link map, and missing or thin sections become regulatory findings. This UC surfaces those gaps months before lodgement so the Company Secretary can drive content-completion.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `cirmp_report_section_lookup` updated through the annual report drafting workflow in the GRC. (2) Schedule UC-22.52.23 weekly during drafting; daily in the final 30 days before lodgement; route `empty_section` and `no_evidence_links` to notable severity=high. (3) Archive every row to `audit_evidence`.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.52.23` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `soci_clause="SOCI-CIRMP-r10"`. Every row pins (entity_id, report_year, rule_id, section_title, section_word_count, evidence_link_count, board_signed_off (true/false), section_status). Dashboard `SOCI — Rule 10 Section Coverage` surfaces empty / thin / unreferenced sections.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Mark a CIRMP rule's section as empty (no evidence link, no word count). UC-22.52.23 MUST flag `section_status="empty_section"`.

### Negative scenario

Complete every CIRMP rule's section with at least one evidence link and content meeting the word-count target. UC-22.52.23 MUST record `section_status="complete"`.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- UC-22.52.22 deployed.
- ES, ServiceNow add-on.
- `index=grc`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookup `cirmp_report_section_lookup` columns: `entity_id`, `report_year`, `rule_id` (4-10 + framework + cross-cutting), `section_title`, `section_word_count`, `evidence_link_count`, `evidence_links_csv`, `board_signed_off` (true/false), `last_edit_at`, `owner`.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Materialise from the GRC annual-report module. Section drafts produce updated rows.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Schedule weekly during drafting; daily in the last 30 days:

```ini
[UC-22.52.23: SOCI Rule 10 section coverage audit]
cron_schedule = 0 12 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.52.23,reg=SOCI,clause=SOCI-CIRMP-r10
action.notable.param.rule_title = SOCI Rule 10 section coverage gap
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

Notable severity tiered by status and proximity to lodgement.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a)** Positive control: set `section_word_count=0`; UC flags `empty_section`.

**(b)** Negative control: complete a substantive section; UC clears to `complete`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `SOCI — Rule 10 Section Coverage`.

**Runbook for `empty_section`** (owner: section owner, severity P2):

1. Within 14 business days: complete the section.

**Runbook for `awaiting_board_signoff`** (owner: Company Secretary, severity P2):

1. Within 30 business days: schedule the board meeting.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Lookup row staleness** — GRC integration delay; ensure the integration runs hourly during drafting.

**Section merger** — ensure `section_merger_lookup` reflects the consolidated structure.

**Product / vendor-specific failure modes**:

- **ServiceNow GRC — word-count counting non-prose text**: the ServiceNow GRC Compliance UI counts every character including tables and embedded artefact URLs; this can mask a thin section. Mitigation: write a ServiceNow Scripted REST API that returns a `prose_word_count` calculated by stripping markdown tables, code blocks, and URLs before counting; the lookup integration consumes this rather than the raw character count.
- **ServiceNow GRC — evidence-link inventory failure mode**: links stored as `sys_attachment` references rather than fully qualified URLs trigger `evidence_link_count=0` for the Splunk view. Mitigation: in the ServiceNow integration, normalise every `sys_attachment` to a permanent `instance.service-now.com/sys_attachment.do?sys_id=...` URL before writing to the lookup.
- **Archer GRC — alternative drafting tool**: Archer stores section content in an attachment field that the Archer REST API does not enumerate without explicit attachment-export scope. Mitigation: enable Archer attachment-export scope on the integration credential and re-test the lookup write.
- **Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x — notable suppression by ESCU regex**: ES 7.x notables matching the title regex `*report*` may be auto-suppressed by an unrelated ESCU rule's `suppression_field=rule_title`. Mitigation: prefix the notable title with `SOCI-` to escape the regex match.

**SOCI-specific failure modes**:

- **Cyber framework section** — the Rule 6(3) framework attestation evidence (UC-22.52.15) must be one of the linked evidence artefacts.
- **Cross-jurisdictional reporting** — if the entity is also covered by EU NIS2 or US CIRCIA, ensure those evidence artefacts are reused where possible to reduce drafting effort.
- **Reportable incidents during reporting period** — Section should reconcile to UC-22.52.5 / 22.52.6 evidence.
- **Drafting tool integration** — the GRC module should produce word counts directly; otherwise pair with a markdown-to-word-count Python script.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup cirmp_report_section_lookup
| eval expected_word_minimum = case(
    rule_id="4", 1500,
    rule_id="5", 1500,
    rule_id="6", 3000,
    rule_id="7", 1500,
    rule_id="8", 1500,
    rule_id="9", 1500,
    1=1, 1000)
| eval section_status = case(
    isnull(section_word_count) OR section_word_count=0, "empty_section",
    section_word_count < expected_word_minimum, "thin_section",
    evidence_link_count=0, "no_evidence_links",
    board_signed_off!="true", "awaiting_board_signoff",
    1=1, "complete")
| stats values(section_title) AS section_title sum(section_word_count) AS total_words sum(evidence_link_count) AS total_evidence_links by entity_id report_year rule_id section_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Section-coverage matrix (entity × rule_id). (Panel 2) Word-count vs target. (Panel 3) Evidence-link inventory. (Panel 4) Last-edit recency.

## Known False Positives

1. **First-draft phase** — sections may legitimately be empty 6+ months before lodgement. Suppression: `report_lifecycle_lookup` carries `drafting_window_start`; tolerate empty sections until `lodgement_due_at - 90d`.

2. **Re-org of report structure** — a section may have been merged with another; word-count is split. Suppression: `section_merger_lookup`.

3. **Evidence in external system** — some evidence is in a confidentiality classification not directly linkable; tolerate `evidence_link_count<3` with explanation.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `soci_22_52_23_exceptions`; max grant 60 days during drafting; max 14 days within 90 days of due date.

## References

- [Security of Critical Infrastructure (Critical Infrastructure Risk Management Program) Rules — Rule 10](https://www.legislation.gov.au/F2023L00112/latest/text)
- [CISC Risk Management Program Guidance — Rule 10 Annual Report Content](https://www.cisc.gov.au/legislation-regulation-and-compliance/cirmp)
- [ASIC Regulatory Guide 247 — Effective Disclosure in an Operating and Financial Review (board-level annual reporting governance)](https://asic.gov.au/regulatory-resources/find-a-document/regulatory-guides/rg-247-effective-disclosure-in-an-operating-and-financial-review/)
